description: This bar chart presents a comparative validation of machine learning predictions against experimental measurements for Vickers microhardness (HV) across three distinct Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel cladding samples fabricated via arc cladding on ASTM A36 steel substrate. The x-axis labels three specimens (Sample 1, Sample 2, Sample 3) with varying compositions: Sample 1 contains 14 wt% Cr and 0.45 wt% C; Sample 2 contains 5.3 wt% Cr, 0.53 wt% C with additions of Ni (1.13%), W (1.19%), and V (0.25%); Sample 3 contains 5.2 wt% Cr, 0.15 wt% C with Ni (1.2%), Mo (1.5%), and V (0.25%). Blue bars denote predicted values generated by the support vector regression (SVR) model incorporating modified solid solution strengthening theory, while pink bars represent experimental microhardness values measured under 1 kg load with 20 s dwell time. Sample 1 shows predicted 521 HV versus experimental 515 HV (1.17% error); Sample 2 shows predicted 490 HV versus experimental 500 HV (2.00% error); Sample 3 shows predicted 415 HV versus experimental 424 HV (2.09% error). Error bars indicate measurement variability. The consistent agreement within 2.1% across all three compositions validates the extreme high accuracy of the machine learning pipeline combining conditional generative adversarial networks (CGANs) for data augmentation, feature engineering focusing on shear modulus and cohesive energy parameters, and Bayesian optimization, demonstrating reliable inverse design capability for high-performance steel coatings.
